title: pedicularis racemosa subsp. racemosa
intro: Pedicularis racemosa subsp. racemosa, also known as the Indian paintbrush or fireweed, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of western North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. Its health benefits include soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and pneumonia. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for medicinal use, and is also used in herbal remedies for its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties.
